description: Network-based gaming console detection with LED color feedback on Raspberry Pi/Linux

platform.os_requirements: Linux with arp, ping commands available

setup: Configure config.json with network settings and LED hardware
pipeline.build: Native Python - no build step required
deploy: Or as systemd service: systemctl start lightchanger (see lightchanger.service)

sync_instructions.description: Guidelines for syncing features between Python (main) and ESP32 branches
principles: Each branch maintains independent implementation appropriate for its platform
Feature parity is goal, not exact code parity
Manifest updates should be mirrored to both branches
Detection logic (MAC_OUI, STATIC_LIST, PORT_PROBE, MDNS) should be consistent
when_to_sync: Adding new supported brands and colors
Fixing bugs that apply to both platforms
UI/UX improvements to web config interface

sync_process.step2: Test thoroughly on that platform (compile + verify)
sync_process.step3: Port logic to other branch (adapt code, not copy-paste)
sync_process.step5: Update both manifests to reflect new feature
sync_process.step6: Commit with descriptive message mentioning both branches

do_not_sync: Language-specific patterns (Python threads vs Arduino loops)

components.main.py: Entry point, GameStateController class, main loop with error recovery
components.led_controller.py: LED color management with factory pattern (FastLED/NeoPixel/RPi_WS281x)
components.colors.py: Shared COLOR_MAP and Color namedtuple definitions
components.scanner.py: Network device discovery via ARP table + static list
components.config_manager.py: Thread-safe singleton config loader with RLock and atomic writes
components.steam_detector.py: Steam detection via port probe or mDNS (zeroconf)
components.web_config.py: HTTP configuration UI with JSON editor and atomic writes

architecture.flow: Scan ARP table -> Verify Liveness (ping) -> Check Steam port -> Determine Priority -> Set LED

detection.device_management: Web UI with JSON editor for static devices and colors

endpoints: GET / - Main configuration UI with JSON editor
POST /save - Save configuration (atomic write via tempfile)
Fields: network_scan_interval_seconds, network_detection_mode, devices_static_list, colors, hardware
security_notes: No authentication - only for trusted networks

deployment.install: sudo cp lightchanger.service /etc/systemd/system/ && systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable --now lightchanger

bug_fixes: esp32.ino - fixed DeviceConfig/Device type mismatch in isValidDevice call
esp32.ino - replaced blocking delay(1000) with millis() non-blocking WiFi reconnect
steam_detector.h - reduced delay(100) to delay(10) between IP probes
led_controller.py - removed unused import json, added module-level logger
requirements.txt - added zeroconf>=4.2.0 as required dependency
improvements: Created colors.py shared module with COLOR_MAP and Color namedtuple
main.py imports COLOR_MAP from colors.py instead of duplicating
Added CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) with py_compile, pytest, ESP32 compile
Expanded README with install, API docs, wiring guide, and CI documentation
